home.subtitle: Your financial obligations, organized.
home.demoSubtitle: All figures are demonstration data. Not a real account.

obligations.title: Obligations
obligations.emptySubtitle: Obligations you add will appear here.

obligationDetail.title: Obligation Detail
obligationDetail.phaseNote: Detailed calculation view coming in a future update.
obligationDetail.notFoundSubtitle: This obligation may have been removed or the link is invalid.
obligationDetail.officialPaymentsNotice: This loan is connected to your bank — only the bank can log payments on it.
obligationDetail.archiveConfirmBody: It will be marked closed and removed from active tracking. You can still view it in your history.
obligationDetail.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently removes the obligation and its history. This cannot be undone.

loanDetail.residualNotBalloonWarning: At the unchanged installment, an estimated balance may remain at the original maturity date.
loanDetail.scheduleChangeNotice: The published rate changes how much of each installment goes to interest and principal. Review the agreed schedule, its final balloon, or compare alternative schedules.

rateImpact.title: Rate Impact
rateImpact.addedCostPendingDetail: This figure isn't available yet — it requires sign-off from the finance team before the app can show a verified number. It isn't loading; there's nothing to wait for.
rateImpact.addedCostEstimateDetail: Estimate, pending finance team sign-off — the extra interest this loan is projected to cost over its full term because of the rate change on file, versus if the original rate had never changed.
rateImpact.unsupported: Rate impact isn't available for this obligation type yet.
rateImpact.residualNotBalloonWarning: This estimated remaining balance is not a contractual balloon or a confirmed final payment. The bank must confirm how it will be handled.
rateImpact.scheduleReviewNotice: The installment remains unchanged, so the updated rate may produce a larger final payment. Open the agreed schedule to see it clearly, or compare the recommended and custom alternatives.

causes.rateIncreaseWithUnchangedInstallment: Your rate increased while your installment stayed the same, so more of each payment covers cost instead of principal.
causes.paymentShortfall: Recent payments have been below the amount needed to fully amortize the loan.
causes.contractualBalloon: This loan's contract includes a scheduled balloon payment at maturity.
causes.unknown: A residual balance is projected, but the specific cause could not be determined from available data.

schedule.title: Agreed Schedule
schedule.unsupported: Schedule isn't available for this obligation type yet.
schedule.residualNotBalloonWarning: The unchanged installment leaves a final balance at maturity.
schedule.finalBalloonNotice: The agreed installment does not fully clear the balance by maturity. The final month clearly includes the remaining balloon amount shown below.
schedule.outdatedNotice: A logged payment or a rate change means the agreed schedule no longer matches reality. Review the recommended schedule.
schedule.pendingProposalBody: There's nothing to show yet because your current contractual schedule has no installments left, and a proposal you submitted is still pending review. It will appear here once decided.

murabahaSchedule.fixedExplainer: The total sale price and monthly installments were fixed when you signed. They never change — there is no interest rate to reprice, and paying early does not reduce the agreed profit.
murabahaSchedule.projectionNote: Balances are projected from your fixed agreed installments, assuming each is paid on schedule.
murabahaSchedule.finalAdjustedBody: The last installment differs slightly so the payments clear the exact agreed sale price — no other installment changes.

scheduleProposal.title: Recommended Schedule
scheduleProposal.nonBindingNotice: This recommended alternative recalculates payments to clear the projected balance by the original maturity date. It is a comparison only and does not change the agreed schedule.
scheduleProposal.projectedRemainingPayable: Projected remaining payable under this proposal
scheduleProposal.unresolvedAtMaturity: Unresolved projected balance at original maturity
scheduleProposal.notBalloonWarning: This amount is shown separately for safety. It is not inserted as a last installment, contractual balloon, or confirmed final payment. The bank must confirm its treatment.
scheduleProposal.acceptedLocally: Sent to the bank for review. Your agreed schedule remains unchanged until the bank approves it.
scheduleProposal.acceptedPersonally: This is now your agreed schedule.
scheduleProposal.invalidInstallment: Enter a valid installment greater than zero.
scheduleProposal.calculationError: The proposal could not be calculated from the available loan data.
scheduleProposal.loadError: The loan or its published rate history could not be loaded.
scheduleProposal.submitError: The proposal could not be sent. Please try again.
scheduleProposal.alreadyPendingError: You already have a pending proposal for this loan. It must be decided before you can submit another.
scheduleProposal.pendingExistsBody: Submitted {{date}} — proposed installment {{installment}} {{currency}}. The bank must review it before you can submit a new one.
scheduleProposal.pendingExistsBodyPersonal: Submitted {{date}} — proposed installment {{installment}} {{currency}}. It must be decided before you can submit a new one.
scheduleProposal.optionLowerInstallmentBody: Keep the original maturity date with a smaller monthly installment.
scheduleProposal.optionSameInstallmentBody: Keep your current installment and finish paying {{months}} months earlier.

scheduleGenerator.title: Custom Schedule Generator
scheduleGenerator.nonBindingNotice: Choose any installment to generate a private comparison. The final month will clearly show any resulting balloon. Nothing here changes the agreed schedule.
scheduleGenerator.finalBalloonNotice: This balloon is part of the generated scenario's final month. It becomes real only if the customer and bank formally agree to this schedule.

scenario.title: Scenario Simulator
scenario.unsupported: The simulator isn't available for this obligation type yet.
scenario.murabahaFixedBody: This is a Murabaha: the profit was fixed when you signed the sale, and there is no interest rate that reprices. Paying extra or early does not reduce the agreed profit, so an extra-payment simulation would not change the outcome. To settle early, ask your bank whether they grant a discretionary rebate (ibra').

bankQuestions.title: Bank Questions
bankQuestions.q1: What is the current outstanding principal and interest on this loan?
bankQuestions.q2: Can you provide the exact calculation for the new installment amount after the recent rate increase?
bankQuestions.q3: If I keep my installment unchanged, what is the estimated balloon payment at maturity?
bankQuestions.q4: Are there any penalties for making extra monthly payments towards the principal?
bankQuestions.q5: Can I extend the loan term to eliminate the residual risk without increasing my monthly installment?

murabahaDetail.earlySettlementBody: Settling this financing before term is possible. Any discount (ibra') on the remaining amount is at the bank's discretion — this app does not simulate or promise one. Ask your bank for their current early-settlement terms.

cardDetail.minimumPaymentCaveat: The minimum payment keeps your account current — it is not the amount that pays off your balance.

cardSimulator.title: Card payoff simulator
cardSimulator.assumption: Estimate uses monthly accrual. Your bank may calculate charges daily.
cardSimulator.neverPaysOff: This payment does not cover the monthly charge, so the balance will not pay off.
cardSimulator.missingData: Add the card APR, balance, and minimum-payment rule before calculating.
cardSimulator.invalidPayment: Enter a payment amount greater than zero, or leave it blank for minimum-only.

onboarding.languageSubtitle: You can change this later in Settings.
onboarding.introBody: Track loans, Islamic financing, and credit cards in one honest place. Every number is labeled by source and confidence.
onboarding.introFeatureProvenance: Every figure labeled by source and confidence
onboarding.introFeatureMath: Honest financial math — no hidden rounding
onboarding.consentBody: This demo uses fictional data for illustrative purposes only. All figures are invented — they do not represent real accounts, real balances, or real financial advice.
onboarding.consentAcknowledge: I understand this is demonstration data
onboarding.personalConsentBody: Your personal account uses authenticated Supabase APIs. Your account and financial records are protected by user-scoped access controls.
onboarding.personalConsentAcknowledge: I acknowledge the privacy policy and consent to storing my personal account data

learn.title: Learn
learn.subtitle: Understand loans, financing, and credit — in plain language.

learn.guideBody: Learn first, then compare published options. Terms may change; confirm final terms with the institution.
learn.snapshotBody: Verified {{date}}. This is a representative snapshot, not a complete market listing.

learn.assistantPrivacy: Eltizamati sends only this conversation and the comparison details you enter here. Your account credentials and stored obligation records are not sent to the AI assistant.

learn.assistantSuggestion2: What's the difference between a loan and Murabaha financing?
learn.assistantSuggestion3: What should I check before signing a financing contract?
learn.assistantSuggestion4: How are credit card minimum payments calculated?
learn.sampleTitle: Sample explanation — not a live AI response
learn.sampleAnswer: Compare the published structure, pricing type, fees, salary-transfer requirement, and what is still unknown. Ask the institution for the final offer and its effective date.
learn.searchEmpty: No education topics match that search.

howLoansWork.title: How a conventional loan works
howLoansWork.body: A conventional loan gives you a principal amount up front, which you repay over time through regular installments. Each installment covers part interest (the bank's charge for lending) and part principal. The amortization schedule shows exactly how that split changes over the life of the loan.

fixedVsVariable.title: Fixed vs. variable rate
fixedVsVariable.body: A fixed rate stays the same for the agreed period, so your installment is predictable. A variable rate can change during the loan — usually through repricing tied to a reference rate — which means your installment or loan term can shift over time.

residualBalance.title: Why a residual balance can appear
residualBalance.body: If your rate increases but your installment doesn't, more of each payment covers interest instead of principal. Over time this can leave a residual balance still owed at maturity, sometimes called a balloon payment. It isn't always a sign of a problem — but it's worth understanding before it surprises you.

earlyRepaymentConventional.title: Paying off a conventional loan early
earlyRepaymentConventional.body: Early repayment means paying part or all of your outstanding balance before the scheduled end date. Banks may apply their own terms or fees to early repayment — always confirm the current terms with your bank before deciding.

howMurabahaWorks.title: How Murabaha financing works
howMurabahaWorks.body: In Murabaha, the bank buys the asset you want and sells it to you at a disclosed markup (profit rate). The total sale price is fixed at signing and split into installments. Because the total is fixed upfront, your outstanding financing amount only ever goes down as you pay, never up.

whyNoInterestInIslamicFinancing.title: Why Murabaha never uses "interest"
whyNoInterestInIslamicFinancing.body: Murabaha is structured as a sale, not a loan — the bank's return is a disclosed profit built into the fixed sale price, not interest charged on a balance. That's why this app never uses the word "interest" anywhere for Murabaha, Ijara, or Diminishing Musharakah.

earlySettlementAndIbra.title: Early settlement and ibra'
earlySettlementAndIbra.body: Settling Islamic financing before the end of its term is called early settlement. A bank may voluntarily grant ibra' — a discount on the remaining amount — but this is entirely at the bank's discretion. This app never simulates or promises an ibra' amount; ask your bank for their current terms.

ijaraAndMusharakah.title: Ijara and Diminishing Musharakah, briefly
ijaraAndMusharakah.body: Ijara is a lease: you pay rent to use an asset the bank owns, often ending in ownership transfer. Diminishing Musharakah is co-ownership: you and the bank both own the asset, and you gradually buy the bank's share while paying rent on the portion it still owns. Both are shown read-only in this app for now.

minimumPaymentVsPayingInFull.title: Minimum payment vs. paying in full
minimumPaymentVsPayingInFull.body: The minimum payment keeps your card account current — it is not the amount that pays off your balance. Paying only the minimum means finance charges keep accruing on the rest. If you pay your full statement balance within the grace period, you typically avoid finance charges altogether.

creditUtilization.title: Credit utilization and why it matters
creditUtilization.body: Utilization is your card balance as a share of your credit limit. Keeping utilization low is generally considered healthier for your finances and your credit profile. This app flags when a card's utilization crosses a high threshold so it doesn't creep up unnoticed.

obligation.definition: Any financial commitment you must repay: a loan, financing, or credit card.

principal.definition: The amount you borrowed, excluding interest/profit.

interest.definition: The cost the bank charges for lending, usually a yearly percentage.

interestRate.definition: The yearly percentage that determines interest.

installment.definition: The regular payment you make, usually monthly.

outstandingBalance.definition: What you still owe right now.

maturityDate.definition: The date the obligation is scheduled to be fully repaid.

variableRate.definition: A rate the bank can change during the loan, usually following a reference rate.

fixedRate.definition: A rate that stays the same for the agreed period.

amortization.definition: How each payment splits between principal and interest over time.

residualBalance.definition: Money still owed at maturity when payments didn't fully repay the loan.

balloonPayment.definition: A large final payment, either by contract design or caused by under-repayment.

earlyRepayment.definition: Paying part or all of the obligation before schedule.

gracePeriod.definition: Days you can pay a card statement in full without finance charges.

minimumPayment.definition: The smallest card payment that keeps the account current — not the amount that pays it off.

utilization.definition: Card balance as a share of the credit limit.

statementBalance.definition: What you owed on the statement date.

financeCharges.definition: Interest and fees a card adds when you don't pay in full.

murabaha.definition: The bank buys the item and sells it to you at a disclosed markup, paid in installments. Total price is fixed at contract.

financingAmount.definition: What remains of the total sale price you owe in Murabaha (or financing generally).

profitRate.definition: The disclosed markup rate used in Islamic financing.

ijara.definition: Lease-based financing: you pay rent for use of an asset the bank owns, often ending in ownership transfer.

diminishingMusharakah.definition: You and the bank co-own the asset; you buy the bank's share over time and pay rent on what it still owns.

earlySettlement.definition: Settling Islamic financing before term; any rebate (ibra') is at the bank's discretion.

ibra.definition: A discount an Islamic bank may voluntarily grant on early settlement.

provenance.definition: Where a number came from: your bank, the credit bureau, your entry, or our estimate.

estimate.definition: A value we calculated from available data — not an official bank figure.

officialValue.definition: A value that came directly from your bank or lender.

repricing.definition: The bank updating a variable rate, usually when a reference rate moves.

delinquency.definition: The state of being behind on a scheduled payment.

dayCount.definition: The rule for counting days when computing interest (e.g. 30/360, actual/365).

apr.definition: The true yearly cost including compounding (and fees, where stated).

about.heading: About Eltizamati and these terms
about.body: Eltizamati is a personal tool for tracking your loans, credit cards, and Islamic financing (such as Murabaha) in Jordan, and understanding how rate changes affect what you owe. It is an informational and organizational tool — not a bank, lender, credit bureau, or licensed financial advisor. By creating an account and ticking the agreement box, you confirm you are at least 18 years old and accept these Terms & Conditions and this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not create an account.

demoVsPersonal.heading: Demonstration mode vs personal mode
demoVsPersonal.body: Demonstration mode uses entirely fictional data to show how the app works; that data is invented, stays on your device for the session, and represents no real account. Personal mode stores the information you enter about your own obligations. These terms govern personal mode; demo mode figures are illustrative only and must never be treated as real financial information.

dataWeStore.heading: What data we store
dataWeStore.body: In demo mode, all data is fictional and lives only on your device for the duration of your session — nothing is sent to any server. In personal mode, your full name, contact phone number, email address, primary bank name, obligations, payments, and rate history are stored in our Supabase database, protected by row-level security so only you — and, where you have obligations a participating institution manages, that institution's authorized operators — can access your records. We never sell your data.

dataSources.heading: Where your numbers come from
dataSources.body: Every figure in this app is labeled by its source: your own manual entry, an official value from your bank, or an estimate we calculated. We never present an estimate as an official figure, and Murabaha profit is always shown as fixed at signing — never re-priced. Bank and open-banking connections are planned but not yet available — see Data Status in Settings for the current, honest state of every connection.

communications.heading: Emails and notifications
communications.body: By agreeing to these terms you consent to receive emails and in-app or push notifications about matters relevant to your account: the status of your loan applications, published rate changes and how they affect your obligations, recommended or agreed schedule proposals, payment reminders you have configured, security and account updates, and occasional service messages. We only send to the email address on file for your account. You can withdraw this consent at any time from Settings, or by using the unsubscribe option in any email; withdrawing it may mean you no longer receive important updates about your obligations.

notAdvice.heading: Not financial or Sharia advice
notAdvice.body: Eltizamati helps you organize and understand your obligations, but it does not provide financial, legal, tax, or Sharia advice, and nothing in the app is an offer of credit or a recommendation to take, settle, or refinance any obligation. Calculations are estimates based on the information available and may differ from your institution's official figures. Always confirm amounts, profit, and settlement terms directly with your bank or financing provider before acting.

security.heading: Security and access
security.body: Your account is protected by authenticated sign-in and database row-level security. Keep your password confidential and sign out on shared devices. No online service can guarantee perfect security; if you believe your account has been accessed without authorization, change your password and contact us promptly.

yourRights.heading: Your rights
yourRights.body: You can review everything you've acknowledged in Settings → Acknowledgments, correct the information you entered, manage your communication preferences, and sign out at any time. You can permanently erase your account and all associated data from Settings → Account → Delete Account.

accountDeletion.heading: Account deletion
accountDeletion.body: Deleting your account permanently erases your profile, obligations, payments, rate history, communication preferences, and consent records. This action cannot be undone.

changes.heading: Changes to these terms
changes.body: We may update these terms as the app evolves. When we make a material change we will raise the version shown above and ask you to review and accept the updated terms before you continue. Your continued use after accepting a new version means you agree to it.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.body: For any question about these terms, your data, or your communication preferences, contact the Eltizamati team through the support option in Settings. We aim to respond to data and privacy requests promptly.

settings.title: Settings
settings.resetDemoConfirmBody: This clears everything you have done in demo mode and restores the original seeded loan, Murabaha, and card. This cannot be undone.
settings.acknowledgmentsEmptySubtitle: Legal acknowledgments you accept will be listed here.
settings.demoAccountPrompt: You're exploring with demo data. Sign in or create an account to save your own.
settings.deleteAccountConfirmBody: This permanently erases your account and all associated data. This cannot be undone.

installmentUnchanged.title: Installment unchanged after rate increase
installmentUnchanged.body: Your installment remains {{installment}} JOD despite the rate increase. Your remaining balance may be higher than expected.

residualRisk.title: Residual balance risk
